text:
BOTTOM LINE — Semis crashed, energy spiked on oil. A violent rotation out of AI hardware into commodities smashed semis, while oil surged on Iran tensions; defensive sectors held, revealing a high-to-low tape shift.

INDICES — VIX 16.1, flat. Rank by return: DIA -0.31%, SPY -0.48%, IWM -0.91%, QQQ -1.85%, SOXX -5.13%. Large > small by 0.43pp. Oil +4.38% was the notable cross-asset mover. QQQ is below its 200-day MA.

VOLUME — SPY vol_ratio 0.9x, QQQ vol_ratio 1.0x. Elevated volume on QQQ and SOXX with price down — distribution in tech. Low volume on SPY with modest loss suggests low-conviction selling outside tech.

SECTORS & ROTATION — XLE Energy +2.84%, XLV Healthcare +1.53%, XLRE Real Estate +1.35%, XLP Staples +0.90%, XLU Utilities +0.88%, XLC Comms +0.73%, XLF Financials -0.16%, XLY Discretionary -0.53%, XLB Materials -0.90%, XLI Industrials -1.71%, XLK Technology -2.39%. Top Industries: USO Oil +4.38%, FCG Natural Gas +3.09%. Bottom: SOXX Semis -5.13%, WGMI Crypto -6.82%. Money rotating out of AI hardware and into energy; call it: AI hardware fading / high-to-low rotation.

POSITIONING — QQQ put-call ratio 1.38 (elevated protection demand). IV skew +13.2% — put premium signals fear. QQQ at $575 vs gamma flip $610 — price below flip, dealer hedging amplifies downside. VIX 16.1 below its 20-day MA of 17.5 — relative complacency.

CROSS-ASSET — Oil +4.38% vs SOXX -5.13%: Iran tensions drove energy up while same headlines amplified chip sell-off, a direct divergence.

SIGNALS & WATCH — Greed at 68.2. Top movers: INTC +11.6% (semis), TER +3.6% (semis), NET -4.4% (cloud). 20d momentum extremes: best sector XLE, worst sector XLK; best basket USO, worst basket SOXX. Vol spike ticker IHI +0.27% on 2.1x vol — low-conviction bounce in medical devices, not accumulation. DeepSeek/Samsung headlines drove the semi rout. Watch: SOXX — -5.13% below gamma flip; INTC — +11.6% on a semi-relief bounce; USO — +4.38% on Iran tensions, trend reversal from 20d low.
